name: hasura-ndc

metadata.name: hasura-ndc
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Hasura Python SDK

To install the library you can use:

`pip3 install hasura-ndc`

Python developers can use this SDK to create Hasura Data Connectors using the Python programming language.

You would be interested in this repository if you wanted to write your own Hasura data connector in Python that wrapped a database or custom data-source.

Included in this SDK is an implementation of a Lambda connector, called a FunctionConnector. If you wanted to simply write Python code you'd likely be more interested in the Python Lambda connector which makes use of this, and you can find that repo here: https://github.com/hasura/ndc-python-lambda

Developer notes:

To upload a new version of the Hasura SDK to pypi:

python3 setup.py sdist

python3 setup.py bdist_wheel

twine upload dist/*

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.BadRequest
documentation: The request did not match the data connector's expectation based on this specification

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.Forbidden
documentation: The request could not be handled because a permission check failed - for example, a mutation might fail because a check constraint was not met

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.Conflict
documentation: The request could not be handled because it would create a conflicting state for the data source - for example, a mutation might fail because a foreign key constraint was not met

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.UnprocessableContent
documentation: The request could not be handled because, while the request was well-formed, it was not semantically correct. For example, a value for a custom scalar type was provided, but with an incorrect type

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.InternalServerError
documentation: The request could not be handled because of an error on the server

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.NotSupported
documentation: The request could not be handled because it relies on an unsupported capability. Note: this ought to indicate an error on the caller side, since the caller should not generate requests which are incompatible with the indicated capabilities

classes.name: hasura_ndc.errors.BadGateway
documentation: The request could not be handled because an upstream service was unavailable or returned an unexpected response, e.g., a connection to a database server failed

functions.name: hasura_ndc.instrumentation.get_exporters
documentation: Get the appropriate exporters based on the protocol.
